MOTION To Strike Objections, Compel Substantive Verified Responses To Requests For Admission, Form Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions From Deft Sheedy Drayage Co.
Off Calendar Sep-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Geraldine Hilt, et al.
RULING:
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to strike objections and to compel further responses to requests for admission and form interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. Within 20 days defendant must provide further responses to requests for admissions 2-8, 12-22 and further responses to interrogatory 17.1 as to requests for admissions 1, 9-11 and 23-24. Defendant's responses to requests for admission 1, 9-11 and 23-24 are sufficient, yet defendant's responses to form interrogatory 17.1 as to those responses are insufficient. The responses to the requests for admissions are sufficient because they contain denials with satisfactory non-evasive explanations why defendant made those denials and/or contain adequate statements of insufficient knowledge to admit or deny the requests. The responses to 17.1 are insufficient because defendant has not answered any of the subparts of that interrogatory as to those responses and its responses to the requests for admissions are not an adequate substitute for answering the subparts of 17.1. Defendant's responses to requests for admission 2-8, 12-22 are insufficient because those responses either include evasive or non-responsive information and/or were responded to under the mistaken assumption that an inability to admit or deny after reasonable inquiry is a permissible basis for a denial. Plaintiffs' motion to strike defendant's general objections is granted, but plaintiffs' motion to strike specific objections is denied. The general objections lack merit and are not in proper form. The specific objections were timely made and are preserved for trial. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Defendant's position did not lack substantial justification. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
